title: semecarpus densiflorus
intro: Semecarpus densiflorus, also known as Marking Nut, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Semecarpus densiflorus include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, and its extracts are also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
